name: Urban Upbound
description_short: Non-profit organization providing free financial counseling, tax prep, employment services, and credit union access to low-income New Yorkers in public housing.
description_long:
Urban Upbound was founded in 2004 as the East River Development Alliance (ERDA) by Debra-Ellen Glickstein and Bishop Mitchell Taylor to address economic inequality in New York City's public housing communities. The organization operates with a mission to break cycles of poverty by providing comprehensive, integrated services to NYCHA residents and other low-income New Yorkers who are systematically excluded from mainstream financial services and economic development opportunities.

The organization offers seven integrated programs: Employment Services (job readiness, vocational training, OSHA certifications), College Access & Youth Development, Mental Health and Wellness, Financial Counseling and Affordable Housing, Tax Preparation and Income Support, Community Revitalization through Business Innovation, and Financial Inclusion services through the Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union. All services use an intensive case management model ensuring clients have access to the full array of offerings. Services are delivered both in-person and virtually in group and individual formats.

Urban Upbound distinguishes itself through its federal credit union anchor, which provides financial products and asset-building tools unavailable through mainstream banks to underserved residents. The organization has demonstrated measurable impact: serving 38,500+ New Yorkers over three years, returning $25.77M in tax refunds to the community, and achieving 100% college acceptance rates for College Access program participants. The integrated service model addresses interconnected barriers—employment, education, financial literacy, mental health, and housing—rather than treating them separately.

As a legitimate non-profit with federal credit union status and NYC Council funding, Urban Upbound represents authentic free financial help with accountability. The primary caveat is geographic and eligibility limitation: services primarily target NYCHA residents and low-income Queens/NYC residents, making it unavailable to most consumers outside these communities. Additionally, while tax prep and credit union services are free, the organization's primary value is in case management and wraparound support rather than traditional credit repair or debt resolution.
pros: Operates Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union, providing credit union loans and financial products specifically designed for underserved residents excluded from mainstream banking
Free tax preparation services that returned $25.77M in refunds to community over three years
100% college acceptance rate for College Access program participants, indicating highly effective youth development
Intensive case management model integrating seven service areas (employment, housing, mental health, tax prep, financial counseling, college access, business innovation)
Provides free vocational training in high-growth sectors including healthcare, security, administration, and culinary arts
OSHA-authorized training provider offering free OSHA 30 and NYC Site Safety Training certifications
Serves 98,000+ NYCHA residents currently lacking access to banking or credit union services
cons: Limited geographic availability: services primarily serve NYCHA residents and low-to-moderate income residents in Queens/NYC, not accessible to most consumers
No traditional credit repair or dispute services offered—focuses on building financial capability rather than fixing existing credit reports
Website lacks detail on credit union membership requirements, APR rates, or specific loan products available
No indication of wait times, service capacity limits, or whether services are currently accepting new clients
Limited information about mental health and wellness program specifics, despite listing it as a core service
best_for: NYCHA public housing residents and low-income New Yorkers seeking comprehensive wraparound financial and employment services
Young adults and students in public housing seeking college access support and career development
Individuals seeking free tax preparation and income support services in NYC
Unbanked or underbanked Queens residents seeking credit union membership and financial products
diagnosis: Urban Upbound is best for NYCHA residents and low-income New Yorkers seeking free, comprehensive financial and employment services anchored by a federal credit union committed to economic inclusion. The main caveat is strict geographic and population limitations—services are designed specifically for public housing residents and low-income NYC residents, making it inaccessible to most consumers outside these communities.
services: Free tax preparation and income support services
Job readiness training (resume writing, interview prep, soft skills)
Vocational training in healthcare, security, administration, culinary arts, and customer service
One-on-one career counseling and job placement services
Job retention support and career advancement services
Free OSHA 30 and NYC 10-hour Site Safety Training certifications
College access and youth development programs
Financial counseling and affordable housing assistance
Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union membership and financial products
Worker cooperative development and business innovation programs
Computer lab access for job searching and virtual training
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
